paper_title: Ice Core and Climate Data Analysis

methods.description: Process for preparing and analyzing ice core samples to measure stable isotopes, tritium concentrations, and electrical conductivity for chronological and environmental studies.

steps.description: Ice core samples with a thickness of 90 mm were cut longitudinally into three parts (thicknesses of 5, 75, and 10 mm) with a band saw.
steps.searchable_summary: cut ice core samples into sections for analysis

steps.description: One of the 5 mm-thick sections was horizontally cut at 25 mm intervals for analysis of the stable isotopes of water.
steps.searchable_summary: cut 5 mm section into 25 mm intervals for isotope analysis

steps.description: The subsamples were placed into clean polyethylene bags, melted at ambient temperature, and decanted into glass bottles with an inner cap.
steps.searchable_summary: melt subsamples and transfer to glass bottles

steps.description: The stable oxygen and hydrogen isotope compositions of water were measured using a water stable isotope analyzer (L2130-I, Piccaro Inc.) with an evaporating device (A0212, Picarro Inc.).
steps.searchable_summary: measure stable isotopes using isotope analyzer

steps.description: Tritium concentrations were measured using a liquid scintillation counter (LSC-LB3, Aloka Co., Ltd.) to identify the reference horizon of the H-bomb test in 1963.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tritium concentrations using scintillation counter

steps.description: The continuous electrical conductivity was measured using a dielectric profiling system (DEP).
A continuous density profile of the ice core was obtained using an X-ray transmission method.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain density profile using X-ray transmission

steps.description: The concentration of Cl−, SO42−, and Na+ were measured by ion chromatography.
steps.searchable_summary: measure ion concentrations using ion chromatography

steps.description: Non-sea-salt (nss) SO42− was calculated by the ratio of Na+ and SO42−.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate nss SO42− from Na+ and SO42− ratios

methods.description: Utilization of climate indices and reanalysis data to understand climate variability and its impact on the ice core data.

steps.description: We used the monthly data set of the NAO index supplied by the Climatic Research Unit, University of East Anglia.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch NAO index data from Climatic Research Unit

steps.description: We used the monthly data set of the AMO index supplied by the Earth System Research Laboratory (ESRL).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch AMO index data from ESRL

steps.description: We estimated the heat flux of the warmer current as the vertically integrated heat transport (VIHT) supplied by the Ocean Analysis/Reanalysis System 3 (ORA-S3), ECMWF.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch VIHT data from ORA-S3 ECMWF

steps.description: The sea ice concentration, precipitation amount, and specific humidity used in this study were from the ERA-interim reanalysis data set supplied by the European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch sea ice concentration, precipitation, and humidity data from ERA-interim
